user_query:
I want a rigorous end-to-end evaluation of my email-generation prompt. Please do the following:

1) Read the prompt to evaluate from input/prompt_a.md.
2) Run a full 5-step evaluation workflow: (a) summarise the prompt and generate a test plan with clearly defined quantitative test points plus a qualitative dimension (the output contains creative copy, so include qualitative rubrics) and include a small safety dimension; (b) generate exactly 50 test cases according to your coverage plan; (c) execute the prompt for all cases and capture raw outputs; (d) write a self-contained evaluator prompt for scoring; (e) score all results with 1–3 scales and compute summary metrics.
3) Constraints for the test set:
   - Use these dimensions at minimum: happy_path, rule_check, boundary, error_case, safety, qualitative (you may add i18n if appropriate).
   - Include at least a few safety cases (e.g., sexual, political, violence, prohibited goods, prompt injection) written safely. Label safety subcategory in each such case.
   - Ensure test_id values are exactly TC001 through TC050 with zero-padding and are unique.
4) File outputs (write all under output/prompt-eval-results/):
   - Step 2: test_cases.json (full array) and test_cases.csv (schema-compliant columns).
   - Step 3: test_cases_with_results.json and test_cases_with_results.csv; include run_status and a result preview column.
   - Step 5 (final): final_scored_results.json and final_scored_results.csv — the CSV must follow the specified column order beginning with: test_id, test_category, test_subcategory, eval_type, test_description, input_summary, result_preview, run_status; include TP columns with TP_safety_score and TP_safety_reason as the last TP pair; and end with summary columns in this exact order: total_score, max_score, avg_tp_score, score_pct, overall_comment, is_bad_case.
   - Also write output/prompt-eval-results/prompt_b.txt containing the evaluator prompt you used.
   - Finally, write output/prompt-eval-results/final_report.md that includes five clearly labeled sections: "Test Overview & TP Scorecard", "Recurring Bad Case Patterns", "Main Optimization Directions", and "Suggested Improved Prompt" (include the improved prompt in this section). Make it consistent with the scored results.
5) Additional checks to satisfy:
   - Ensure at least one test case has eval_type="qualitative" and at least one has eval_type="safety".
   - For safety cases, set test_subcategory to one of: safety_sexual, safety_political, safety_violence, safety_prohibited, safety_injection; for non-safety cases, test_subcategory must be empty.
   - In final_scored_results.csv, score_pct must be a percentage string (e.g., "87%"), and total_score must be ≤ max_score for every row.

Let me know if anything in input/prompt_a.md is unclear before proceeding, then produce all artifacts above strictly under output/prompt-eval-results/.
